mission.title: Install and Configure Trivy Operator (Aqua Security)
mission.description: The Trivy Operator is a Kubernetes-native security toolkit that continuously scans your cluster for vulnerabilities and compliance issues. It automatically generates security reports as Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) in response to changes in the cluster. This operator is ideal for enhancing security posture but may introduce overhead due to continuous scanning.

steps.title: Add the Aqua Security Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official Aqua Security Helm repository to your Helm configuration, allowing you to install the Trivy Operator.

```bash

helm repo add aquasecurity https://aquasecurity.github.io/trivy-operator

helm repo update

```

> This command adds the repository for the Trivy Operator.
> Updates the local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install the Trivy Operator
steps.description:
This step installs the Trivy Operator using Helm with the specified version and namespace.

```bash

kubectl create namespace trivy-system

helm install trivy-operator aquasecurity/trivy-operator --namespace trivy-system --version 0.32.1

```

> Creates a namespace for the Trivy Operator.
> Installs the Trivy Operator in the trivy-system namespace.

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
This step checks if the Trivy Operator pods are running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n trivy-system

```

> Lists all pods in the trivy-system namespace to verify the installation.

steps.title: Create a sample Custom Resource
steps.description:
This step creates a sample Custom Resource to trigger a security scan.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: trivy.aquasecurity.github.io/v1alpha1
kind: VulnerabilityReport
metadata:
  name: sample-vulnerability-report
  namespace: default
spec:
  resource:
    apiVersion: v1
    kind: Pod
    name: sample-pod
EOF

```

> Creates a sample VulnerabilityReport CR.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: trivy.aquasecurity.github.io/v1alpha1
kind: VulnerabilityReport
metadata:
  name: sample-vulnerability-report
  namespace: default
spec:
  resource:
    apiVersion: v1
    kind: Pod
    name: sample-pod
EOF

steps.title: Check the status of the Custom Resource
steps.description:
This step verifies that the Custom Resource is ready and the scan has been initiated.

```bash

kubectl get vulnerabilityreports -n default

```

> Lists all VulnerabilityReports in the default namespace to check the status.

uninstall.title: Uninstall the Trivy Operator
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Trivy Operator and its associated resources. Be aware that this will delete all security reports and configurations associated with the operator.

```bash

helm uninstall trivy-operator --namespace trivy-system

kubectl delete namespace trivy-system

```

> Uninstalls the Trivy Operator from the specified namespace.
> Deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the Trivy Operator
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up any important configurations. This step upgrades the Trivy Operator to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n trivy-system -o json > trivy-backup.json

helm upgrade trivy-operator aquasecurity/trivy-operator --namespace trivy-system --version 0.32.1

kubectl get pods -n trivy-system

```

> Backs up the current state of the Trivy Operator pods.
> Upgrades the Trivy Operator to the specified version.
> Verifies that the pods are running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get pods -n trivy-system -o json > trivy-backup.json

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to create resource: the server could not find the requested resource'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Custom Resource Definition (CRD) may not be installed correctly.

**Fix:**
Reinstall the CRDs using 'kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/aquasecurity/trivy-operator/main/deploy/crds/trivy.aquasecurity.github.io_vulnerabilityreports.yaml'.

troubleshooting.title: Pods in 'trivy-system' namespace are in 'CrashLoopBackOff' state.
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Trivy Operator may be misconfigured or missing required permissions.

**Fix:**
Check the logs of the pod using 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n trivy-system' for details.

troubleshooting.title: No security reports generated after deploying a new pod.
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Trivy Operator may not be watching the correct namespace or resource types.

**Fix:**
Ensure the operator is configured to watch the appropriate namespaces and resources.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Unable to connect to the server: dial tcp <ip>:<port>: connect: connection refused'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Kubernetes API server may be down or unreachable.

**Fix:**
Check the status of the Kubernetes cluster and ensure the API server is running.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new compliance reports including NSA and CISA Kubernetes Hardening Guidance.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the output of 'kubectl get vulnerabilityreports -n default' showing the status of the created VulnerabilityReport as 'Ready'.
codeSnippets:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: trivy.aquasecurity.github.io/v1alpha1
kind: VulnerabilityReport
metadata:
  name: sample-vulnerability-report
  namespace: default
spec:
  resource:
    apiVersion: v1
    kind: Pod
    name: sample-pod
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with the required resources and tools installed.
